segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of an embroidery hoop, displaying intricately stitched leaf patterns in various green and beige tones, each labeled with Korean and English names like 'lazy daisy stitch,' 'bullion stitch,' 'satin stitch,' 'blanket stitch,' 'fly stitch,' and 'granitos stitch.' Gentle, calming background music featuring piano and strings plays throughout the segment. The camera slowly pans across the hoop, highlighting the texture and detail of each stitch before transitioning to a hand holding the completed embroidery against a soft blue background. A female voice introduces herself as 'Luna's Day' (루나의 하루), announcing her intention to demonstrate various embroidery techniques, specifically the 'outline stitch' and 'stem stitch.' The scene then shifts to a new embroidery hoop with faint blue outlines of leaf patterns, indicating the start of a new project. The speaker reiterates her plan to demonstrate the 'outline stitch' and 'stem stitch.' The focus narrows to a close-up demonstration where a needle, threaded with green embroidery floss, executes the stem stitch along a pre-drawn line. The speaker provides clear instructions in Korean, saying, 'Pull the needle up through the fabric,' followed by the distinct sound of the needle piercing the fabric. She continues, 'Keep the thread above,' and with another needle sound, 'Make one stitch forward.' On-screen text in both Korean and English labels the 'outline stitch' and 'stem stitch,' reinforcing her spoken instructions. She advises, 'At this time, don't pull the thread all the way out,' as the demonstration continues with precise, looping motions. The bright and even lighting consistently emphasizes the fine details of the thread and fabric texture.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute begins with gentle, calming background music as a close-up view shows a hand skillfully demonstrating the stem stitch on white, textured fabric within a wooden embroidery hoop. A female voice, speaking in Korean, provides clear instructions, stating "중간에서 바늘을 뽑아줍니다" (Pull the needle out from the middle) and "실을 위로하고" (With the thread upwards) as the needle, threaded with green floss, enters and exits the fabric along a pre-drawn blue dotted line labeled '스템 스티치'. The rhythmic, subtle sounds of the needle pulling through the fabric accompany the precise movements. The speaker continues, guiding the viewer to "앞으로 한 땀 놓아주고" (Place one stitch forward), reinforcing the methodical process. Around the 0:34 mark, the speaker announces, "다음은 스템 스티치를 놓아보겠습니다" (Next, let's try the stem stitch), and then explains the technique, noting that "스템 스티치는 아웃라인 스티치와 하는 방법이 같은데요" (The stem stitch is done the same way as the outline stitch), but clarifies the difference: "아웃라인 스티치는 실을 위로하고 스티치를 해 주었다면 스템 스티치는 실을 아래로 하고 스티치 해 줍니다" (If the outline stitch was done with the thread upwards, the stem stitch is done with the thread downwards). Previously completed red outline stitches and purple stem stitches, also labeled bilingually, are visible above the current work. The demonstration continues with the speaker instructing to "바늘을 뽑아주고" (Pull the needle out) and "실을 아래로 하고" (With the thread downwards), as the hand adjusts the hoop and the green thread forms a twisted, rope-like stitch, progressing steadily along the line. The bright, even lighting highlights the fabric's texture and the controlled motion, with the speaker's final instruction, "앞으로 한 땀" (One stitch forward), cut off as the minute ends.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a hand demonstrating the stem stitch on white, textured fabric held taut in a wooden embroidery hoop. Gentle, calming instrumental music plays in the background as a female voice, speaking Korean, provides clear instructions. On-screen text clearly labels the '스템 스티치' (stem stitch) in both Korean and English, positioned below a previously completed green '아웃라인 스티치'. The hand, visible by its fingers and thumb, precisely guides a needle threaded with green embroidery floss along a pre-drawn blue dotted line. The distinct sound of the needle piercing the fabric is heard as the demonstrator instructs, "At this time, don't pull out all the thread," and "Pull out the needle from the middle." The needle enters and exits the fabric in a consistent, diagonal motion, creating a twisted, rope-like stitch. The speaker continues, "With the thread underneath, place one stitch forward," and again, "Pull out the needle from the middle," as the hand adjusts thread tension and needle angle, ensuring even spacing and tight loops. As the stem stitch nears completion, the hand pauses, leaving the needle in place. The speaker then explains, "The outline stitch and stem stitch are done in the same way. The name can change and the twist can change depending on whether you place the thread above or below." She adds, "Usually, for a line that bulges upwards, you use the outline stitch, and for a line that bulges downwards..." The segment concludes showing the completed green stem stitch line parallel to the earlier outline stitch, both neatly aligned within the blue-outlined rectangular area, maintaining a calm and instructional atmosphere.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a completed embroidery section, showcasing various green and beige leaf patterns stitched on white fabric within a wooden hoop, while on-screen text identifies the stitch types in Korean and English. A gentle, calming background music begins to play. The scene transitions to a new embroidery project on a fresh blue-outlined embroidery hoop, where faint blue outlines of leaf patterns are visible on the white fabric. A female voice, speaking in Korean, introduces the technique, stating, "스탬 스티치를 사용합니다" (We use the stem stitch). A hand enters the frame, holding a needle threaded with beige embroidery floss, and begins demonstrating the 'outline (stem) stitch' along a pre-drawn blue line. The female voice provides precise instructions in Korean, guiding the process: "줄기 끝에서 바늘을 뽑아주고 실을 밑으로 합니다" (Pull the needle from the end of the stem and put the thread underneath), followed by "앞으로 한 땀 놓아주고" (Place one stitch forward), as the needle moves in and out of the fabric with a distinct pulling sound. The on-screen text remains visible, labeling the stitch as '가지: 아웃라인(스템) 스티치' and 'outline(stem) stitch'. The voice continues, "중간에서 바늘을 뽑아줍니다" (Pull the needle from the middle), again accompanied by the sound of the needle, and adds, "이때 실을 전부 잡아당겨 줍니다" (At this time, pull all the thread). The hand methodically continues the stitching, creating a defined, raised line that follows the pre-drawn guide, emphasizing the technique's precision and consistency. The background music continues as the voice reiterates instructions like "앞으로 한 땀 놓아주고" and "중간에서 바늘을 뽑아줍니다," each action punctuated by the sound of the needle and thread. The bright, even lighting highlights the fabric's texture and the emerging stitch.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a serene close-up of a hand expertly demonstrating the stem stitch on white fabric, held taut within a wooden embroidery hoop. Gentle, calming instrumental music plays continuously in the background, setting a peaceful tone. The hand, visible from the thumb and fingers, deftly manipulates a needle threaded with beige embroidery floss, following faint blue pre-drawn lines that outline a botanical design. Each precise stitch is formed by inserting the needle slightly ahead and bringing it up just behind the previous one, creating a smooth, continuous, overlapping line that mimics a natural stem. Subtle rustling sounds accompany the hand's movements as it adjusts the fabric and hoop, ensuring even tension and precise alignment. Occasionally, a distinct click or clack can be heard, likely from the needle interacting with the hoop or fabric. On-screen text, presented in both Korean ('가지: 아웃라인(스템) 스티치') and English ('outline(stem) stitch'), reinforces the educational focus of the demonstration. The bright, even lighting highlights the fabric's texture and the emerging beige threadwork, maintaining a tight focus on the intricate process of embroidery.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a blue-outlined embroidery hoop holding white fabric, where faint blue outlines of leaf patterns are visible. On-screen text, identifying the stitch as 'lazy daisy stitch' in both Korean and English, is prominently displayed. Gentle, calm instrumental music begins, accompanying a female voice speaking in Korean, "레이지데이지 스티치로 작은 잎들을 수놓아 보겠습니다." (Let's embroider small leaves with lazy daisy stitch.) A hand enters the frame, holding a needle threaded with beige embroidery floss, and begins stitching along the pre-drawn blue lines. The narrator guides the process, instructing, "바늘을 뽑아주고" (Pull out the needle), followed by the distinct sound of the needle pulling through the fabric. The hand then brings the thread up as instructed, "실을 위로 하고," and inserts the needle at the starting point, "시작점으로 바늘을 넣어서." The voice continues, "앞으로 한 땀 떠 줍니다." (Make one stitch forward.) The hand carefully guides the needle in and out of the fabric, securing each loop with precision to create individual looped stitches, forming small, petal-like shapes that mimic leaves. The narrator reiterates, "그리고 바늘을 뽑아 줍니다." (And pull out the needle.) As the stitching progresses, the previously completed sections show multiple lazy daisy stitches forming textured, greenish-beige leaf shapes. The hand adjusts the fabric and needle with deliberate, controlled movements, ensuring each stitch follows the pre-drawn outline accurately. The voice further instructs, "앞쪽으로 바늘을 넣어서" (Insert the needle forward) and "고리를 고정해 줍니다." (Fix the loop.) The fabric remains taut in the hoop, and the lighting highlights the texture of the weave and the sheen of the thread. The completed stitches appear as small, rounded loops, each anchored at the base, creating a natural, organic look. The on-screen text remains visible throughout, reinforcing the stitch name also spoken by the narrator. The female voice concludes, "다시 한번 해볼게요." (Let's try it one more time.), maintaining the overall calm and focused atmosphere that emphasizes the delicate artistry of hand embroidery, all set against the continuous gentle music.

Finally, the voice instructs to "secure the loop" (고리를 고정해줍니다) as a small, looped petal is formed. This repetitive process creates multiple petals per leaf, with the hand precisely adjusting the tension and angle of the needle. Throughout the segment, the on-screen text '레이지데이지 스티치' and 'lazy daisy stitch' remains consistently visible in the upper right corner. The bright, even lighting highlights the fabric's weave and the emerging texture of the beige stitches, which gradually form a cluster of looped leaves along a stem. The rhythmic sounds of the needle and fabric continue as the hand works on the next set of petals, showcasing the delicate and repetitive nature of the lazy daisy stitch.

segments.integrated_caption: The video maintains a close-up on the detailed embroidery process, demonstrating the bullion stitch on white fabric held taut in a wooden hoop. A human hand, holding a needle threaded with light green embroidery floss, meticulously wraps the thread multiple times around the needle to form a thick, coiled stitch along a pre-drawn blue leaf outline. A clear female voice provides precise instructions in Korean, guiding the viewer through each step, stating "바늘을 뽑아 줍니다" (Pulls out the needle), "밑으로 바늘을 넣어 줍니다" (Puts the needle underneath), and "시작점 옆으로 바늘을 다시 한번 뽑아주고" (Pulls out the needle again next to the starting point). Subtle clicking sounds accompany the hand's deliberate movements as the needle is guided through the fabric, securing the raised, textured bullion stitch. The voice continues, instructing to "바늘이 나왔던 구멍으로 넣어주고" (Puts it into the hole where the needle came out) and "꽃잎 끝점에서 바늘을 뽑아서 앞으로 넣어 줍니다" (Pulls out the needle from the tip of the petal and puts it forward). On-screen text updates to '불리온 스티치 bullion stitch', reinforcing the technique being demonstrated. The speaker then explains, "그러면 불리온 스티치를 레이지 데이지 스티치가 감싸는 형태가 되는데요" (Then the lazy daisy stitch wraps around the bullion stitch), adding that it's "도톰하고 작은 잎들에 사용하면 좋을 듯합니다" (good to use for plump and small leaves). Previously completed lazy daisy and stem stitches are visible, providing context for the evolving botanical pattern, as the hand's steady movements showcase the skill required for consistent tension and alignment. The speaker concludes by stating, "다시 한번 해볼게요" (Let's try it one more time), indicating a repetition of the technique.

The hand then wraps the thread around the needle multiple times, creating a coiled, textured effect that builds up the leaf’s shape. Another sound of the needle pulling through the fabric is heard as the hand reinserts the needle at the same point to secure the coil, forming a raised, dense stitch. The narrator reiterates, "At this time, make the thread go to the right." The hand moves with precision, following the pre-drawn blue lines, gradually filling the leaf with the granitos stitch, while the background music continues. The narrator concludes, "Once again, pull out the needle from the starting point," as the needle sound is heard again, emphasizing the detailed, methodical process of creating the textured leaf.

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment presents a close-up, continuous demonstration of the granitos stitch being applied to a leaf design on white fabric stretched in an embroidery hoop, all set to a backdrop of calming instrumental music. A female voice, speaking in Korean, guides the process, instructing the viewer to "insert into the same hole" as a hand, holding a needle threaded with light green embroidery floss, meticulously executes the stitch. The speaker further directs to "make the thread go upwards, to the right" and to "pull the needle out from the starting point one more time," as the hand precisely wraps the thread around the needle and secures it with insertions, forming a raised, coiled texture that mimics a leaf. Faint blue pre-drawn stitch lines on the fabric guide the hand, which then "inserts the needle" and "arranges the thread neatly." The on-screen text consistently identifies the technique as '그라니토스 스티치 granitos stitch' in both Korean and English, a label reinforced by the speaker who concludes, "pass it through three or four times like this to complete the granitos stitch." The fabric remains taut and well-lit, highlighting the weave and the emerging stitch, while previously completed lower leaves in varying green shades provide context. The hand moves with controlled precision, completing one coiled segment at a time, gradually building the leaf’s form, with subtle clicks and taps occasionally heard as the needle works through the fabric. The overall atmosphere is calm and focused, emphasizing the delicate artistry of hand embroidery.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of a hand meticulously executing the granitos stitch on white, textured fabric stretched in a wooden hoop, following pre-drawn blue leaf outlines. Gentle, calm piano music plays throughout, accompanied by the subtle sounds of the needle pulling thread through the fabric. On-screen text identifies the stitch as '그라니토스 스티치 granitos stitch'. The camera then pans slightly to reveal a broader view of the embroidery project, showcasing multiple completed leaf designs using various stitches. A female voice then clearly states in Korean, "다음은 플라이 스티치를 해볼게요." (Next, I'll try the fly stitch.) The hand begins the fly stitch on a new blue-outlined fern-like pattern with darker green thread, and the on-screen text updates to '플라이 스티치 fly stitch'. The speaker continues, instructing, "입 끝에다 바늘을 뽑아줍니다." (Pull the needle out at the tip of the leaf.) The hand precisely follows, forming small V-shaped stitches. Further instructions are given: "그리고 밑으로 스트레이트 스티치를 놓아줍니다." (And then, make a straight stitch downwards.) The hand continues to build the intricate design, with the speaker guiding, "왼쪽에서 바늘을 뽑아주고 실을 밑으로 하고 오른쪽으로 바늘을 넣어 준 후 가운데에서 스트레이트 스티치가 끝나는" (Pull the needle out from the left, put the thread underneath, insert the needle to the right, and then in the middle, where the straight stitch ends...), as the audio fades, emphasizing the artistry and precision of the hand embroidery.
